pattern: Split the four arrays into two pairs, precompute all pairwise sums for the first pair in a hash map, then count complement matches from the second pair.
hints: A direct O(n^4) loop over all index combinations is too slow; find a way to reduce the search space by pairing the arrays.
Precompute every possible sum of one element from nums1 and one from nums2, storing counts in a map. Then for each pair from nums3/nums4 look up how often their negation appeared.
Build a frequency map of all nums1[i]+nums2[j] sums. For each nums3[k]+nums4[l], add freq[-(nums3[k]+nums4[l])] to the answer.

edge_inputs.description: small example with two tuples summing to zero
single element in each array all zero
all arrays identical with repeats, many valid index combinations
single element in each, sum is exactly zero
large values that would overflow 32-bit if multiplied

stress.description: moderate n with values spread across the constraint range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size
    a = [rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    b = [rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    c = [rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    d = [rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    return [a, b, c, d]
stress.note: narrow value range to maximise zero-sum hit rate
